channels.functional_purpose: Receives DICOM instances, derives routing metadata from the calling and called AETs, and submits each instance to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor. Compatible Zeiss IOLMaster instances are also written to the local IOLMaster input folder.
channels.business_process: Ingest imaging and route supported IOLMaster biometry for import.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

channels.ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after source transformation and responds after processing.
main_filters: No source filter; IOLMaster file destination is removed unless manufacturer is Zeiss, model contains IOLMaster, and SOP Class UID is supported.
main_transformer_stages: Read local and remote AET values.
Derive identifier type and institution ID from the local AET or configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType.
Set source_platform from ImageNet or Forum AET hints.
Extract DICOM header fields and remove the IOLMaster destination when not compatible.
channels.error_handling: No destination queueing or configured destination retries.
channels.retry_behaviour: Destination retryCount is 0.
channels.queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000; destination queues are disabled.
channels.logging: No explicit logging steps are configured; DICOM content and attachments are stored.

channels.functional_purpose: Reads supported document files from a local drop folder, parses document metadata from an eight-part filename, and submits the binary document as a document_upload_request to the PayloadProcessor.

main_filters: Accepts only pdf, jpg, jpeg, png, gif, mp4, mpeg4, ogg, webm, or mov files with an eight-part double-underscore filename.

channels.functional_purpose: Receives inbound HL7v2 messages by MLLP and selectively updates OpenEyes patient, appointment, and patient-merge resources through PASAPI.
channels.business_process: Synchronize patient demographics, appointments, and patient merges from the PAS into OpenEyes.
channels.ack_behaviour: MLLP listener auto-generates its response before processing; configured HL7 acknowledgements are AA, AE, or AR.
main_filters: Patient update excludes M05.
Patient merge accepts A40.
Disabled appointment delete accepts A38.
main_transformer_stages: Normalize doubled quotes in the inbound HL7 XML representation.
Create PASAPI patient XML and map hospital number, visit, clinic, doctor, status, and NHS number values.
Build PatientAppointment XML and PatientMerge XML for filtered destinations.
channels.error_handling: No configured destination queueing or application retries.
channels.retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations have retryCount 0.
channels.queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and HTTP destination queues are disabled.
channels.logging: No explicit logging steps are configured; channel metadata stores source and type.

channels.functional_purpose: Accepts HTTP requests, converts either a patient identifier lookup to QBP^Q21 or supplied XML to an ADT message, sends the message to the remote PAS by MLLP, and returns an XML patient-list response when available.
channels.business_process: Support OpenEyes-initiated PAS demographic queries and PAS status updates.
channels.ack_behaviour: The HTTP source responds after processing with destination d6; the Respond JavaScript Writer returns TransformedResponse or an empty PatientList element.
main_filters: Send Q21 requires PV1.3.2 or QPD.3, or an A03 message trigger.
main_transformer_stages: Set MSH timestamp and message ID.
Map hosnum or nhsnum to QPD.3.
Convert supplied XML request data to ADT segments and map location status values, otherwise create QBP^Q21.
Convert returned K21 data to XML and form the HTTP response.
channels.error_handling: If no transformed response exists, returns an empty PatientList element; no destination queueing is configured.
channels.retry_behaviour: TCP Sender retryCount is 0; MLLP transport maxRetries is 2.
channels.queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000 and all destinations have queues disabled.
channels.logging: Transformer logs segment indexes, request JSON, location validation failures, and DG1 data, creating a PII-in-log risk.

channels.functional_purpose: Processes PAS-origin HL7v2 patient, merge, referral, appointment, and did-not-attend events and submits mapped XML to OpenEyes PASAPI. It also decodes ReportFile OBX content into a Mirth attachment.
channels.business_process: Patient demographic synchronisation, patient merge/referral handling, clinic appointment status maintenance, and did-not-attend recording for the Newmedica practices.
channels.source_system: Upstream Mirth channel carrying PAS-origin HL7v2
channels.ack_behaviour: No network ACK is produced by this Channel Reader source.
main_filters: Secondary patient and patient merge destinations accept A40 only.
Appointment PUT accepts A01, A02, A03, A04, A05, A11, A12, A13, S12, S13, S14, or S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
Appointment DELETE accepts A38 or S15.
Did-not-attend accepts S26 and rejects ReportFile OBX.
OBX attachment destination accepts ReportFile; its Cataract filter is disabled.
main_transformer_stages: Map practice identifier from PV1 location, transform HL7 demographics to PASAPI Patient XML, and derive hospital identifiers.
Parse PASAPI patient response, mark actionable warnings as errors, and store PatientId in message-scoped channelMap.
Map referral, appointment, appointment status, merge, and did-not-attend payload values from HL7 segments.
Decode ReportFile OBX payloads into Mirth attachments.
channels.error_handling: Transformer scripts catch mapping exceptions and log errors. PASAPI patient response warnings other than missing GP or Practice set the response status to error.
channels.retry_behaviour: Destination retryCount is 0 with a 10000 ms configured retry interval.
channels.queue_behaviour: Source buffer size is 1000; all destination queues are disabled.
channels.logging: Logs transformer mapping exceptions and a debug message for OBX document handling. The channel stores production message content, so operational logs/message storage can contain PII.

evidence.claim: Channel Reader accepts HL7v2 from an upstream Mirth channel; enabled HTTP destinations send patient, referral, appointment, merge, and did-not-attend data to OpenEyes PASAPI V1 and V3.

channels.functional_purpose: Receives PAS ADT messages over MLLP and updates OpenEyes patients and appointments through PASAPI V1. It separately handles patient merges and appointment cancellation/deletion events.
channels.business_process: Synchronising patient demographics, encounters, appointments, and merges from the PAS into OpenEyes.
channels.ack_behaviour: The TCP HL7 response generator is configured for AA success, AE error, and AR rejection acknowledgements.
main_filters: PASAPI - Secondary Patient and PASAPI - Patient are split by A40 versus non-M05 events.
Clinic List - PUT accepts A01, A05, A08, A11, A03, A13, A02, and A12.
Clinic List - DELETE accepts A38.
PASAPI - Patient Merge accepts A40.
main_transformer_stages: Extract hospital/NHS identifiers, visit details, clinic and doctor codes, appointment status, and merge identifiers from HL7.
Transform patient data to PASAPI XML and PUT patient records.
PUT or DELETE PASAPI appointments according to ADT event filters, and PUT patient merges.
channels.error_handling: Patient response transformers strip server log text, inspect warnings, and set responseErrorMessage; mapping scripts log extraction errors.
channels.retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0 (retry interval configured as 10000 ms).
channels.queue_behaviour: Destination queues are disabled; no source queue is configured.
channels.logging: Mapping scripts log errors and one DoctorCode script logs PD1.4.1, creating a PII-in-log risk.
